## Decision 902: Fixture -- injected repo_state predicate fires (Decided)

**Status:** Decided
**Date:** 2020-01-01

**Reversal conditions:**
This fixture exists to drive tests/test_decision_conditions.py's injected-fixture-predicate
"fired" case. `always-true-fixture-predicate` is never registered in the production
_PREDICATE_REGISTRY -- the test injects it via evaluate(predicates={...}), never mutating the
production registry. review_by is far in the future so a "fired" result can only come from the
predicate, proving fired takes priority over not-due.

```yaml reversal-conditions
decision: 902
review_by: 2099-01-01
on_trigger: "re-decide via /plan; update or re-arm this stanza"
conditions:
  - id: always-fires
    kind: repo_state
    predicate: always-true-fixture-predicate
    params: {}
    description: "fixture predicate that always returns True, for the fired-state unit case"
```
